title: Development of GaN-based devices for high power and high frequency electronics
author: Andrzej Taube
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: GaN-based devices, due to the excellent electrical properties of gallium nitride and related materials, such as the high value of the critical electric field, wide bandgap, and high mobility and saturation velocity of two-dimensional electron gas carriers in AlGaN/GaN quantum wells, are widely used in high-power and high-frequency electronics. In this work we present the results of development of GaN-based transistor both for high power and microwave electronics carried out at Lukasiewicz Research Network-Institute of Microelectronics and Photonics. We have developed key technological steps i.e. regrown ohmic contacts for devices on bulk semi-insulating GaN substrates, selective etching of p-GaN layers over AlGaN, deposition of proper passivation layer as well as thermally stable isolation of adjacent devices using selective Fe+ ion implantation, which were integrated in the process flow of manufacturing of high power transistors. We have shown measurements of developed microwave transistors on bulk GaN substrates as well as normally-off p-GaN gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT power transistors assembled using in-house developed process in TO-220 package. Finally, ongoing works on development of vertical GaN power transistors will be presented.
